Name: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
Event: Friday Stories and Glories: World Mythology (Ages 5–8)
Description: An interactive in-gallery program for children ages 5–8 exploring stories, mythology, and folklore from around the world. Activities include storytelling and hands-on gallery exploration as part of an ongoing series.
Address: 1000 5th Ave, New York, NY 10028, USA

Name: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
Description: A program connecting art and literature for children ages 9–12 through readings, role-playing, sketching, and discussion inspired by works in The Met’s collection. This is part of an ongoing multi-week series and typically requires registration.
Address: 1000 5th Ave, New York, NY 10028, USA

Name: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
Description: An evening program for teens (ages 15–18) to be creative, socialize, and participate in art-making, performances, and activities across the Museum. Teen Fridays often include snacks, workshops, and opportunities to meet peers and museum staff.
Address: 1000 5th Ave, New York, NY 10028, USA

Cost: Free (for New Yorkers with valid ID)
Address: 180 Greenwich St, New York, NY 10007, USA

Name: Museum of Broadway
Event: SIX: The Musical Dance Class with Elizabeth Racanelli
Description: This Friday, get down with Elizabeth Racanelli, resident choreographer of SIX on Broadway! This all-levels class has very limited spots - reserve yours today.
Address: 145 W 45th St, New York, NY 10036, USA

Name: Gilder Center for Science, Education, and Innovation
Event: A Night at the Museum: The Overnight Experience
Description: An after-hours overnight program for kids ages 6–12 and their chaperones featuring flashlight tours of halls, scavenger hunts, games, and activities including group games in the Richard Gilder Center. Participants explore exhibitions and enjoy evening programming before sleeping in Museum spaces.
Address: 415 Columbus Ave, New York, NY 10024, USA

Name: Gilder Center for Science, Education, and Innovation
Description: Exclusive off-hours access for Members and their guests to explore the Museum’s exhibitions without the crowds during monthly evening hours. Access is available for members and includes entry through the Gilder Center at 79th Street and Columbus Avenue.
Address: 415 Columbus Ave, New York, NY 10024, USA

Name: Merchant's House Museum
Event: Killing an Evening with Edgar Allan Poe
Description: A one-man, candlelit performance of Edgar Allan Poe’s stories and poems by John Kevin Jones presented in the museum’s double parlor. Select performances offer a pre-show “Raise a Glass with Edgar” reception in the 19th-century kitchen.
Address: 29 E 4th St, New York, NY 10003, USA

Name: Banksy Museum New York
Description: An evening stencil workshop where you’ll learn street-art techniques, create a take-home piece, and enjoy music and wine; materials and guidance are provided. ([museumbanksy.com](https://museumbanksy.com/upcoming-events/sip-and-stencil))
Address: 277 Canal St, New York, NY 10013, USA

Name: Banksy Museum New York
Description: Weekly Monday film screenings (times include 2PM, 4PM, and 6PM); films rotate week-to-week and admission is free with a full-priced museum ticket (screening-only tickets available). ([museumbanksy.com](https://museumbanksy.com/upcoming-events/monday-movie-night-banksy-does-new-york120825/))
Address: 277 Canal St, New York, NY 10013, USA

Address: 277 Canal St, New York, NY 10013, USA

Name: Banksy Museum New York
Description: Every Tuesday evening from 5–7 PM the museum offers Happy Hour — a complimentary glass of wine or beer with full-priced admission; must be 21+. ([museumbanksy.com](https://museumbanksy.com/upcoming-events/happyhour21-6/))
Address: 277 Canal St, New York, NY 10013, USA

Name: Poster House
Description: A members-only guided tour of the Act Black exhibition, led by a docent who will highlight posters and themes from Black American stage and screen. The tour offers in-depth context and close-looking for members.
Address: 119 W 23rd St, New York, NY 10011, USA

Name: Poster House
Event: Virtual Vibrant Verbal Description Tour: Molly Crabapple
Description: A virtual vibrant verbal description tour focused on Molly Crabapple’s work, providing detailed audio descriptions and contextual commentary for remote audiences. This session is intended to make the exhibition accessible to people who are blind or have low vision.
Address: 119 W 23rd St, New York, NY 10011, USA

Name: Poster House
Description: A hands-on monotype printmaking workshop held as part of Poster House’s First Friday programming where participants create unique monotype prints under instructor guidance. Materials and instruction are provided during the session.
Address: 119 W 23rd St, New York, NY 10011, USA

Name: Poster House
Event: First Friday: Max Fractal Performance Live at Poster House
Description: A live performance by Max Fractal presented as part of Poster House’s First Friday evening programming. The event features a live set/performance inside the museum setting.
Address: 119 W 23rd St, New York, NY 10011, USA

Name: Poster House
Event: Members-Only Tour: 100 Years of the Schomburg Center
Description: A members-only guided tour exploring the materials and narratives related to the 100 Years of the Schomburg Center, offering historical context and highlights from the collection. The tour is led by a museum docent for Poster House members.
Address: 119 W 23rd St, New York, NY 10011, USA

Name: The Morgan Library & Museum
Event: Colors of the World: Professional Development Workshop for Educators
Description: An on-site professional development workshop for educators exploring medieval and Renaissance manuscripts with hands-on art-making (grinding pigments, mixing watercolors, applying gold leaf) and creation of an original painted initial. Participants are eligible for CTLE credits and invited to apply for the Morgan Book Project.
Address: 225 Madison Ave, New York, NY 10016, USA

Name: The Morgan Library & Museum
Description: Weekly Free Friday Evenings offering free access to the Morgan’s galleries during extended evening hours; reservations are required and tickets are released in advance. A chance to visit exhibitions and enjoy the museum after hours.
Address: 225 Madison Ave, New York, NY 10016, USA

Name: The Morgan Library & Museum
Description: Weekly Free Friday Evenings offering free access to the Morgan’s galleries during extended evening hours; reservations are required and tickets are released in advance. A chance to visit exhibitions and enjoy the museum after hours.
Address: 225 Madison Ave, New York, NY 10016, USA

Address: 1 American Dream Wy b 152, East Rutherford, NJ 07073, USA

Name: Intrepid Museum
Description: Sleepover on the legendary Intrepid with exclusive night-time access to the ship and Space Shuttle Pavilion, including flashlight tours, unlimited simulator rides, hands-on activities, and meals. Intended for youth (ages 5–17) and includes dinner, snack, breakfast, and a 60-day return pass.
Address: Pier 86, W 46th St, New York, NY 10036, USA

Name: American Museum of Natural History
Event: A Night at the Museum: The Overnight Experience
Description: An overnight program for kids ages 6–12 and their chaperones where the Museum reopens after hours for flashlight tours of halls, games, scavenger hunts, presentations, and evening snacks.
Address: 200 Central Prk W, New York, NY 10024, USA

Name: American Museum of Natural History
Description: Off-hours access exclusively for Members to explore exhibitions without the crowds during monthly evening hours (access available to all membership levels).
Address: 200 Central Prk W, New York, NY 10024, USA

Name: American Museum of Natural History
Description: A 21+ Milstein Discovery Series talk (free with RSVP) exploring why only about 42 fish species are endothermic and the evolutionary competition between fishes and marine mammals; drinks available for purchase.
Address: 200 Central Prk W, New York, NY 10024, USA

Name: American Museum of Natural History
Event: James Arthur Lecture: Grandmother Hypothesis and the Evolution of Human Brains
Description: Kristin Hawkes discusses the coevolution of human brains and our unusually long post-reproductive lifespan, presenting evidence and theory behind the Grandmother Hypothesis.
Address: 200 Central Prk W, New York, NY 10024, USA

Name: The Frick Collection
Description: A participatory, discussion-based program hosted by the Frick Art Research Library centered on the book Marie Antoinette's Head by Will Bashor. This session is held online via Zoom (event listing notes it is currently full).
Address: 1 E 70th St, New York, NY 10021, USA

Name: The Frick Collection
Event: Gallery Talk: A Home for Art
Description: Join a museum educator to learn how the Frick mansion became The Frick Collection in a brief gallery talk. Meet in the Library Gallery for this short, interpretive presentation.
Cost: Free with museum admission; no registration required
Address: 1 E 70th St, New York, NY 10021, USA

Name: The Frick Collection
Event: Gallery Talk: Closer Look at Gainsborough's "Mall in St. James's Park"
Description: A brief gallery talk focusing closely on Thomas Gainsborough’s "Mall in St. James's Park." Meet an educator in the Dining Room for focused discussion of the work.
Cost: Free with museum admission; no registration required
Address: 1 E 70th St, New York, NY 10021, USA

Name: The Frick Collection
Description: Free evening of art and culture with after-hours gallery access, talks, live music in the Garden Court, drop-in drawing, and more. Advance timed tickets are recommended/required for entry; programming runs in the evening.
Address: 1 E 70th St, New York, NY 10021, USA

Name: The Frick Collection
Description: A presentation by Giulio Dalvit, Associate Curator, on his publication Vecchietta followed by a light reception. This program is for Fellows and Young Fellows and requires RSVP.
Address: 1 E 70th St, New York, NY 10021, USA

Name: The Frick Collection
Event: Gallery Talk: A Home for Art
Description: Join a museum educator to learn how the Frick mansion became The Frick Collection in a brief gallery talk. Please meet in the Library Gallery.
Cost: Free with museum admission; no registration required
Address: 1 E 70th St, New York, NY 10021, USA

Name: The Frick Collection
Event: Gallery Talk: Closer Look at David's "Comtesse Daru"
Description: A brief, focused gallery talk on Jacques-Louis David's "Comtesse Daru." Meet an educator in the East Gallery for close looking and discussion.
Cost: Free with museum admission; no registration required
Address: 1 E 70th St, New York, NY 10021, USA

Name: The Frick Collection
Event: Continue the Conversation for Members: Bellini's "St. Francis in the Desert"
Description: An online, members-only conversation about Bellini’s "St. Francis in the Desert." This session is offered via Zoom for members to engage further with the work.
Address: 1 E 70th St, New York, NY 10021, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Event: Lumière, Le Cinéma! 2025. Directed by Thierry Frémaux
Description: Thierry Frémaux’s 2025 documentary traces the birth of cinema through restorations of more than 100 early films accompanied by a period-inflected score. The program offers a historical, archival exploration of the Lumière brothers’ pioneering work.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Description: A relaxed, weekly drop-in art-making program for LGBTQ+ high school students and allies that provides supplies, snacks, and a welcoming space to create. The session is informal and encourages experimentation and community building.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Description: Mani Kaul’s 1983 documentary is a contemplative study of the ancient North Indian classical music tradition Dhrupad, featuring performances by the Dagar family and filmed at historic sites. The film emphasizes sound, space, and meditative imagery over conventional narrative.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Description: Akira Kurosawa’s 1985 reimagining of King Lear set in feudal Japan, noted for its epic scale, dramatic battle scenes, and powerful performances. The film is a monumental spectacle of power, betrayal, and tragedy.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Description: Late-night Fridays at MoMA featuring extended gallery hours, talks, activities, and films; part of MoMA’s Art For All initiative. Admission is free for New York State residents with prior reservation; other visitors may attend with a paid ticket.
Cost: Free for New York State residents (reserve required); paid for others
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Description: A facilitated, relaxed drawing session held in Gallery 210 with artist-led demos and drawing prompts; materials and seating are provided and participation is open to all. Sessions are family-friendly and available in English and Spanish.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Description: A brief 15-minute introduction led by MoMA staff in the Agnes Gund Garden Lobby to help visitors navigate and enjoy their evening at the museum. These quick orientations are timed to coincide with late-night Friday programming.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Event: A Summer at Grandpa’s (冬冬的假期). 1984. Directed by Hou Hsiao-hsien
Description: Hou Hsiao-hsien’s 1984 film from the Taiwan New Cinema movement follows two siblings sent to the countryside and unfolds through loosely connected vignettes and long, observational takes. The film emphasizes naturalistic rhythms and the subtleties of everyday life.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Event: Almanac of Fall (Öszi almanach aka Autumn Almanac). 1984. Directed by Béla Tarr
Description: Béla Tarr’s 1984 chamber piece, shot in color, confines action largely to a dilapidated apartment and uses bold lighting and probing compositions to explore despair and moral exhaustion. The film marks an early stylistic shift in Tarr’s work.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Event: Die Hard. 1988. Directed by John McTiernan
Description: John McTiernan’s 1988 action classic stars Bruce Willis as NY detective John McClane and blends intense set-piece action with sharp character dynamics. The film is widely regarded as a defining blueprint for modern action cinema.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Description: Miloš Forman’s 1984 film dramatizes Antonio Salieri’s fraught relationship with Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart in a lush, award-winning portrait of genius, rivalry, and psychological drama. The screening features the original theatrical cut.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Description: Bette Gordon’s 1983 film follows a young woman who takes a job at a Times Square porno theater and becomes obsessed with a patron, blending New York realism with stylized, neon-lit atmospheres. The film engages themes of desire and urban experience in the waning days of second-wave feminism.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Event: Veronika Voss (Die Sehnsucht der Veronika Voss). 1982. Directed by Rainer Werner Fassbinder
Description: Fassbinder’s 1982 film, inspired by Sunset Blvd., follows a faded film star in 1955 Munich and serves as a critique of postwar German society. The work is notable for its elegiac tone and high-contrast cinematography.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Description: Susan Seidelman’s 1982 debut follows a punkish New Jersey teen pursuing fame in Downtown New York’s scene, blending gritty realism with stylistic indie sensibilities. The film was an early American indie success on the international festival circuit.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Description: A special tribute evening honoring filmmaker and writer Amos Poe, featuring memory, moving image, and music in celebration of his work following his death in December 2025. Colleagues, friends, and family gather for the tribute program.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Event: Die Hard. 1988. Directed by John McTiernan
Description: John McTiernan’s action classic starring Bruce Willis returns for an additional screening; the film is noted for its suspenseful set pieces and charismatic lead performance. This screening is part of the museum’s film programming.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Event: Almanac of Fall (Öszi almanach aka Autumn Almanac). 1984. Directed by Béla Tarr
Description: Béla Tarr’s claustrophobic apartment drama, noted for expressive color and stark psychological tension, appears again as part of the museum’s film series. The work highlights Tarr’s evolving cinematic style.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Event: The Last Emperor. 1987. Directed by Bernardo Bertolucci
Description: Bernardo Bertolucci’s epic biography of Pu Yi, China’s last emperor, traces his life from the Forbidden City through abdication and later obscurity, using cinematic transitions to underscore decline and historical change. The film won nine Academy Awards and is screened in 35mm.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Description: MoMA’s late-night Friday program with extended gallery hours, talks, activities, and film screenings; part of the Art For All initiative that offers free admission to New York State residents with reservations. The program encourages evening engagement with exhibitions and public programs.
Cost: Free for New York State residents (reserve required); paid for others
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Description: Evening drawing sessions facilitated by a teaching artist with materials and seating provided; participants can explore artworks through guided prompts and short demos. This program typically runs as part of UNIQLO Friday Nights.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Event: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom. 1984. Directed by Steven Spielberg
Description: Steven Spielberg’s 1984 prequel features Indiana Jones in a South Asia–set adventure that mixes thrills, dark humor, and memorable set pieces; the screening is presented in 35mm as part of the film series. The film is notable for its impact on MPAA rating practices and popular culture.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Event: Las Madres: The Mothers of Plaza de Mayo. 1985. Directed by Susana Muñoz, Lourdes Portillo
Description: This 1985 documentary chronicles the courageous weekly protests of Argentine mothers seeking the fate of their disappeared children during the Dirty War, using archival footage and interviews to trace their transformation into political icons. The film is an intimate and powerful record of resistance and memory.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: The Museum of Modern Art
Event: Fanny and Alexander. 1982. Directed by Ingmar Bergman
Description: Ingmar Bergman’s richly textured, semi-autobiographical family saga charts childhood idyllic warmth turned to tragedy, rendered through luminous cinematography and expansive storytelling. The film won multiple Academy Awards and is presented in 35mm.
Address: 11 W 53rd St, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: Museum of the City of New York
Description: A conversation about nineteenth-century Spanish-language print culture in New York, tracing networks of Cuban émigrés, political exiles, and Puerto Rican intellectuals who shaped public discourse. The program is moderated by MCNY’s Curator of Community Histories and includes a special performance by Urayoán Noel.
Address: 1220 5th Ave, New York, NY 10029, USA

Name: RiseNY
Event: RiseNY: A Soaring Journey Through New York
Description: An immersive attraction featuring a short documentary film, seven interactive galleries exploring NYC history and culture, and a multisensory 4D flyover ride that lifts guests above a projection dome to ‘soar’ over the city.
Address: 160 W 45th St, New York, NY 10036, USA

Event: RiseNY: A Soaring Journey Through New York

Name: Hayden Planetarium
Description: An immersive 24-minute space show that uses authentic data from NASA, ESA, JAXA, and other missions to explore our galaxy via the Hayden Planetarium’s high-resolution projection and audio system.
Address: 200 Central Prk W, New York, NY 10024, USA

Event: Encounters in the Milky Way (Hayden Planetarium Space Show)
Description: A 24-minute immersive planetarium space show that journeys through the Milky Way, visualizing cosmic movements that shape our galactic neighborhood; narrated by Pedro Pascal.
Cost: Additional ticket required. Free for Members.
Address: 200 Central Prk W, New York, NY 10024, USA

Address: 2 E 91st St, New York, NY 10128, USA

Cost: General Admission: $10 | MOCA Member Admission: Free
Address: 215 Centre St, New York, NY 10013, USA

Description: An in-person family program featuring hands-on games and activities that explore chance, patterns, and probability through playful challenges and demonstrations. Designed for participants of all ages to learn about randomness and better decision-making in a fun, interactive setting.
Address: 635 6th Ave, New York, NY 10011, USA
